label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking what we can infer from the passage. The options are A, B, C, D. Let me read through the passage again to make sure I understand all the details. First, the passage starts by talking about Cinderella and how there's a hidden Cinderella in everyone's heart. Then it introduces Mary Santiago, who has a secret dream. The story is set in a US high school. Mary is shy and loves dancing, but is invisible compared to other girls. Her world changes when a famous teen pop singer, Joey Parker, appears. Joey is kind, handsome, and desirable, unlike the other boys. They meet at a ball, fall in love, but Mary has to leave quickly, leaving her MP3 player behind, which Joey uses to find her. The stepmother is Dominique Blatt, who treats Mary like a maid and wants to prevent her from getting into a top dance school. The two daughters also try to stop Joey from falling for Mary. The story follows Cinderella but adds modern twists. The movie is different from other high school films because it doesn't focus on looks and has less materialism. A movie critic says the focus is on following your dream. Now the question is what we can infer. Let's look at the options: A) Joey is just like other boys in Mary's class. But the passage says Joey is everything the other boys are not—kind, handsome, desirable. So A is false. So not A. B) Mary's mother influences her a lot in singing. The passage mentions Mary's mother died and that Dominique took her in. But there's no mention of her mother's influence on singing. The passage says Mary loves to dance. It doesn't say her mother influenced her in singing. So B might not be correct. C) Not many people have a dream to be realized. The first paragraph says "there is a hidden Cinderella in everyone's heart—we all wish..." which implies that many people do have that dream. So C is the opposite of what's stated. So C is incorrect. D) The MP3 player helps
